sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: NextNav (NASDAQ:NN) Trading Up 5.5% - Here's Why
feed.summary: NextNav (NASDAQ:NN) shares rose 5.5% on Wednesday, trading at $15.1190. Despite the price increase, trading volume was down 63%, and the company remains unprofitable with a market capitalization of $2.04 billion. Analyst ratings are mixed, with some upgrades to 'hold' but a MarketBeat consensus of 'Reduce', while insider activity shows both significant purchases and sales.

feed.title: Honeywell collaborates with SAF One and Tata Projects to enable SAF production
feed.summary: Honeywell has announced a collaboration with SAF One and Tata Projects to facilitate the production of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF). This partnership aims to advance the availability and adoption of SAF, contributing to reduced carbon emissions in the aviation industry. The initiative highlights a joint effort to develop and implement solutions for sustainable fuel production.

feed.title: Franklin Q1 Earnings Coming Up: Here's What to Expect From the Stock
feed.summary: Franklin Resources Inc. (BEN) is set to report its first-quarter fiscal 2026 results on January 30, 2026, with anticipated declines in both earnings and revenues compared to the previous year. Despite favorable market conditions and a slight AUM increase, the company is expected to record net outflows, and a negative Earnings ESP combined with a Zacks Rank of 3 suggests a low probability of beating estimates. Recent developments include a partnership with Wand AI for enterprise-scale AI deployment and the acquisition of Apera Asset Management, which expanded BEN's alternative credit AUM.

feed.title: Here's What Investors Must Know Ahead of Weyerhaeuser's Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser Company (WY) is set to report its Q4 2025 results on January 29, with expectations of continued challenges in the housing and wood products environment. Analysts project a loss per share of 13 cents and a 7.2% year-over-year decline in net sales to $1.58 billion. The company's Wood Products and Timberlands segments are anticipated to see mixed results, influenced by softer demand and pricing issues, while the Real Estate segment is expected to show healthy underlying demand.

feed.title: Ryanair Earnings Came Ahead of Estimates in Q3, Revenues Up Y/Y
feed.summary: Ryanair Holdings plc reported strong fiscal Q3 2026 earnings, beating analyst estimates with 26 cents per share, though revenues of $3.74 billion slightly missed expectations but grew 18.5% year-over-year. The airline saw a 6% increase in traffic to 47.5 million passengers and projects a 4% traffic growth for fiscal 2026 to almost 208 million passengers. Despite increased operating costs from ATC and environmental fees, fuel hedge savings and efficient cost control are expected to maintain modest unit cost inflation, leading to projected full-year profits after tax between €2.13bn and €2.23bn.

feed.title: 3 Reasons Why Growth Investors Shouldn't Overlook Stantec (STN)
feed.summary: Stantec (STN) is presented as a strong growth stock due to its favorable Zacks Growth Style Score and top Zacks Rank. The article highlights three key factors: strong projected earnings growth of 13.3% for the current year, superior year-over-year cash flow growth of 17.2%, and promising upward revisions in current-year earnings estimates. These indicators suggest that Stantec is a potential outperformer for growth investors.

feed.title: Exclusive: Novo's Wegovy and Ozempic US advertising spend doubles rival Eli Lilly, data shows
authors: Harshita Mary Varghese and Patrick Wingrove
feed.summary: Novo Nordisk significantly outspent Eli Lilly on U.S. advertising for its GLP-1 drugs Wegovy and Ozempic, shelling out nearly $500 million in the first nine months of 2025, more than double Lilly’s spend on Zepbound and Mounjaro. This aggressive advertising push by Novo Nordisk, particularly for Wegovy, is seen as an effort to counter Eli Lilly's Zepbound, which showed superior weight loss results in a head-to-head trial and has since overtaken Wegovy in U.S. prescriptions for obesity drugs. The increased ad spending by both companies reflects improved drug availability after earlier shortages and highlights the competitive landscape in the GLP-1 market.

feed.title: Apple, Visa, KLA-Tencor and more set to report earnings Thursday
feed.summary: This article highlights major companies scheduled to report earnings on Thursday, January 29, 2026. Tech giants like Apple, Visa, and KLA-Tencor, along with Western Digital and Stryker, are set to release their financial results after the market closes. The article also provides extensive lists of companies reporting earnings before the open and after the close, including estimated EPS and revenue for each.

feed.title: JKHY: DA Davidson Maintains 'Buy' Rating with $190 Price Target
feed.summary: DA Davidson has reiterated its 'Buy' rating for Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) with a consistent price target of $190, signaling confidence in the company's market position. This decision comes despite recent fluctuations in ratings from other firms, with the average analyst target price suggesting a 6.27% upside from its current price. Jack Henry & Associates is a leading provider of core processing and financial services for small and midsize US banks and credit unions.

feed.title: Sysco (SYS) Soars 11% on Upbeat Outlook
feed.summary: Sysco Corp. (NYSE:SYS) surged 11% after announcing an updated optimistic outlook for fiscal year 2026, driven by strong second-quarter sales. The company expects to reach the high end of its EPS guidance of $4.50 to $4.60, with sales increasing by 3% to $20.76 billion in Q2, despite a slight dip in net income. CEO Kevin Hourican attributed the strong performance to increased local case growth and gross margin expansion, reaffirming confidence in the company's initiatives.

feed.title: Western Digital’s Stock Boosted by Strategic Price Target Increases​
feed.summary: Western Digital Corporation (WDC) has seen a significant boost in its stock price, trading up by 9.25% due to strategic business mergers and numerous analyst price target increases. Mizuho, Citi, Evercore ISI, Barclays, and Rosenblatt have all raised their price targets, citing strong DRAM and NAND market expectations, AI server demand, data center investments, strategic SanDisk monetization, and advanced HAMR technology. These factors, alongside solid financial performance, underscore WDC's resilience and growth potential in the evolving tech landscape.

feed.title: Textron Aviation Logs Record Revenues, Strong Q4 Recovery
feed.summary: Textron Aviation achieved record revenues and a strong recovery in the fourth quarter, marked by a significant 53% surge in jet deliveries. This performance underscores the company's robust operational rebound and growing market presence. The article highlights these financial achievements and the factors contributing to their success.

feed.title: The Analyst Verdict: Becton Dickinson In The Eyes Of 7 Experts
feed.summary: Seven analysts have recently provided ratings for Becton Dickinson (NYSE: BDX), resulting in a bullish to indifferent sentiment breakdown. The average 12-month price target for BDX has increased to $205.57, with individual targets ranging from $190.00 to $215.00. The article also provides a financial performance breakdown, highlighting Becton Dickinson's above-average market capitalization and strong net margin, though it notes challenges in revenue growth and returns on equity and assets compared to industry peers.

feed.title: Plans for Target in Dripping Springs unveiled
feed.summary: Retail giant Target Corp. has acquired property along U.S. Highway 290 in Dripping Springs and has revealed a timeline for the store's opening. This development is significant for other businesses looking to establish themselves near the new Target location. The article also mentions that a lot of shop and restaurant space is still available around the Target site.

feed.title: Expert Outlook: Zimmer Biomet Holdings Through The Eyes Of 13 Analysts
feed.summary: Thirteen analysts have recently provided ratings for Zimmer Biomet Holdings (NYSE: ZBH), with an average 12-month price target of $101.08, reflecting a 3.94% decrease from the previous average. While the company exhibits a solid revenue growth of 9.71% and a strong net margin of 11.54%, its market capitalization is below industry averages, and its ROE and ROA are underperforming. Analysts' opinions range from bullish to bearish, with recent adjustments to ratings and price targets providing insights into their perspectives on the company's financial health and market position.

feed.title: A compelling chart pattern is emerging in this railroad stock
feed.summary: CSX Corporation (CSX) is showing compelling bullish chart patterns across multiple timeframes, including a daily "back-and-forth" trend with higher highs and higher lows, and a larger weekly cup-and-handle breakout. This suggests potential for significant upside movement, with analysts targeting near 43 and 48, making it a noteworthy stock despite the broader market's focus on other catalysts. The stock's recovery from last year's downturn and its current technical setup indicate a constructive development for investors.

feed.title: NTCA Tells FCC to Block Nexstar, Tegna Deal
feed.summary: The NTCA—The Rural Broadband Association has urged the FCC to block the proposed merger of Nexstar and Tegna, arguing it would lead to higher retransmission consent fees. The organization contends that increased fees would disproportionately affect rural communities by forcing consumers to pay higher prices for television services, thereby hindering public interest and rural American services. NTCA cited survey data showing a substantial increase in retransmission costs for rural video providers, with 87% of these providers passing the costs directly to subscribers.

feed.title: ‘The year of Hershey’s’: Inside the confectioner’s first brand platform in 8 years
feed.summary: Hershey's is launching its first new brand campaign in eight years, titled "Hershey’s. It’s Your Happy Place," to coincide with a year full of major cultural events like the Winter Games, Super Bowl, World Cup, and America 250. The campaign leverages the company's long-standing sponsorship of Team USA, featuring Olympic and Paralympic athletes in spots that emphasize happiness beyond winning. This strategic timing aims to connect the brand with moments of global camaraderie and emotional resonance.

feed.title: Hedge Fund and Insider Trading News: Ray Dalio, Warren Buffett, Mala Gaonkar, Citadel Investment Group, BlueCrest Capital Mgmt, Point72 Asset Management, Brevan Howard Asset Management, Streamex Corp (STEX), AutoZone Inc (AZO), and More
feed.summary: This article provides a roundup of recent hedge fund and insider trading news, featuring prominent figures and firms like Ray Dalio, Warren Buffett, Bridgewater, Citadel, BlueCrest, Point72, and Brevan Howard. It covers topics such as stock divestments, investment strategies, entry into new markets, tax disputes, and significant insider trading activities, including sales at AutoZone Inc (AZO) and purchases at Streamex Corp (STEX). The article also touches upon the performance of AI stocks and offers a report on a promising, undervalued AI stock.

feed.title: KLA (KLAC) stock price slips as traders brace for earnings after ASML’s AI-order surge
feed.summary: KLA (KLAC) stock price slipped as traders awaited its fiscal second-quarter earnings report, which follows a surge in AI-driven orders for ASML, a key player in the chip manufacturing sector. KLA's results are expected to set the tone for the chip-equipment industry, as investors look for clear signals on demand amidst high expectations for AI-driven growth. The company’s forecast indicates revenue of around $3.225 billion and non-GAAP earnings of $8.70 per share, which will be closely scrutinized for any signs of caution that could trigger profit-taking.

feed.title: Northern Trust Universe Data: Global Markets Deliver Steady Fourth Quarter Gains to U.S. Institutional Investors
feed.summary: Global markets provided institutional investors with steady gains in the fourth quarter of 2025, with the Northern Trust All Funds Over $100 Million universe reporting a median return of 2.1% for the quarter and 13.1% for the year. This performance was driven by slowing inflation, strong corporate earnings, and robust U.S. GDP growth, despite concerns about AI valuations and initial U.S. government shutdown impacts. Different plan types, including Corporate (ERISA), Public Funds, and Foundations & Endowments, showed varying but positive returns, with fixed income delivering modest gains following Federal Reserve interest rate cuts.

feed.title: Lam Research and Generac have been highlighted as Zacks Bull and Bear of the Day
feed.summary: Zacks Equity Research has named Lam Research (LRCX) as its Bull of the Day due to its strong position in wafer fabrication equipment, particularly its etch and deposition dominance crucial for next-generation chip manufacturing and high-bandwidth memory. Conversely, Generac Holdings (GNRC) is designated as the Bear of the Day, facing challenges from flat revenue growth projections, declining profits, and weak demand for home standby generators, despite some opportunity in data center power solutions. The article also provides a bullish outlook on Micron Technology (MU) due to surging demand for its HBM chips and attractive valuation.

feed.title: SLB Secures Five-Year Technology Deals in Oman's Largest Oil Concession
feed.summary: SLB has secured two five-year contracts from Petroleum Development Oman (PDO) to supply wellheads and artificial lift systems for operations in Block-6, Oman's largest oil and gas concession. These deals aim to boost recovery rates, extend field life, and improve operational efficiency through advanced recovery technologies and in-country value initiatives, including local manufacturing and job creation. The agreement reinforces SLB's role as a technology partner in Oman as the country seeks to maximize output from mature assets.

feed.title: Target Refreshes In-Store Beauty Experience
feed.summary: Target is overhauling its in-store beauty departments with a new layout designed to make product discovery easier, focusing on trends, needs, and exclusive items. This refresh also includes the addition of almost 3,000 new beauty products, making it their largest-ever spring beauty assortment, with more than 90% of items priced under $20. The move comes as Target prepares to conclude its partnership with Ulta Beauty in August 2026, underlining its commitment to independently strengthening its beauty offerings.

feed.title: Procter & Gamble Shifts Toward Premium Growth And Consumer Health Expansion
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble is strategically moving towards premium products and expanding its consumer health division, exemplified by double-digit growth in China's baby care market with premium offerings and the acquisition of the digestive health brand Wonderbelly. This shift indicates a broader strategy to increase value and pricing power across its portfolio. Investors should monitor the global rollout of premium product strategies and the integration of new acquisitions into P&G's core business.

feed.title: Grasberg Restart Plans Meet Strong Freeport-McMoRan Momentum And Valuation Debate
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) is implementing a phased restart of its Grasberg mine in Indonesia after production disruptions, a key asset for copper and gold. The stock is currently trading above analyst targets but is considered undervalued by Simply Wall St, exhibiting strong recent momentum. Key considerations for investors include monitoring production guidance and costs, while noting recent insider selling as a potential risk.

feed.title: Does Ulta’s Middle East and Wellness Push Redefine the Bull Case For Ulta Beauty (ULTA)?
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty is expanding its market reach by launching stores in the Middle East, starting with a UAE store opening in January 2026, and by broadening its product offerings into wellness with initiatives like Wellness by Ulta boutiques. While these expansions are long-term growth avenues, the article suggests they aren't expected to significantly impact near-term financial performance compared to core U.S. trends. The stock's current valuation shows a wide range of opinions among investors, indicating potential sensitivity to execution in these new ventures.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Cancels Entire Cruise Due to Major Technical Issue
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean's Anthem of the Seas cruise had its 10-night New Zealand trip canceled due to a technical issue that caused a significant delay in its return to Sydney. Affected guests will receive full refunds, future cruise credits, and allowances for flights and hotel accommodations. The exact nature of the technical issue, which caused the ship to travel at a reduced speed, has not been disclosed.

feed.title: Fund Update: 253,725 U S BAN (USB) shares added to OLD NATIONAL BANCORP |IN| portfolio
feed.summary: OLD NATIONAL BANCORP (IN) has increased its holdings in U S BAN (USB) by adding 253,725 shares to its portfolio, as reported in a recent SEC 13F filing for the 12-31-2025 report period. The article also details significant hedge fund activity, insider trading, congressional stock trading, and analyst ratings/price targets for USB. Overall, institutional investors show mixed sentiment, while insiders and Congress members have primarily engaged in selling USB stock.

feed.title: 5 Multiline Insurers to Watch Amid Prudent Underwriting and Pricing
feed.summary: The multiline insurance industry is poised for growth due to product diversification, better pricing, prudent underwriting, and increased exposure coupled with faster economic recovery. Key trends include diversified portfolios reducing concentration risk, continued merger and acquisition activities driven by digitalization, and increased adoption of technology like AI and blockchain to enhance operations and customer experience. Five specific insurers—American International Group (AIG), Prudential Financial (PRU), Markel Group (MKL), Principal Financial Group (PFG), and Everest Group (EG)—are highlighted as stocks to watch due to their strategic positioning and growth prospects.

feed.title: Petrobras renews oil deals with Indian refiners worth more than $3.1 bln
feed.summary: Brazil's state-run oil firm Petrobras has renewed and expanded its oil sales contracts with Indian state-owned oil refiners, including Indian Oil Corporation, Bharat Petroleum Corporation, and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation. These agreements have the potential for sales of up to 60 million barrels, valued at over $3.1 billion, and will remain active until March 2027.

feed.title: Debunking Winter Energy Myths
feed.summary: This article debunks common winter energy myths to help homeowners save money and stay warm efficiently. It addresses misconceptions about thermostat settings, closing vents, space heater efficiency, ceiling fan use, dishwashing, curtain management, and primary sources of heat loss, providing practical advice for energy conservation.

feed.title: Qorvo (QRVO) Analyst Ratings Update: Benchmark Reiterates Hold
feed.summary: Benchmark analyst Cody Acree has reiterated a "Hold" rating for Qorvo (QRVO), with no change to the price target. This comes amidst several other analyst updates in late 2025 and early 2026, where ratings largely remained "Neutral" or "Hold," and some price targets were adjusted downwards. The consensus among 19 analysts places Qorvo's average target price at $99.41, suggesting a potential upside of 28.11% from its current price, aligning with a general "Hold" recommendation from brokerage firms.

feed.title: Presidio Property Trust suspends preferred stock dividend
feed.summary: Presidio Property Trust (NASDAQ:SQFT) has suspended its monthly preferred stock dividend, effective January 2026, due to challenging market conditions and financial struggles. The suspension is expected to save the company $2.3 million annually as it grapples with a low Price/Book ratio, negative EPS, and a weak financial health score. The REIT plans to focus on its Model Home portfolio, reduce expenses, and will reassess dividend payments quarterly.

feed.title: Middlesex Water Company (NASDAQ:MSEX) Declares $0.36 Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Middlesex Water Company (NASDAQ:MSEX) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.36 per share, to be paid on March 2nd to shareholders of record by February 17th. This dividend represents a 2.8% annual yield and marks the company's 52nd consecutive year of dividend increases, with the payout comfortably covered by earnings. Despite missing recent earnings estimates, analysts have a "Moderate Buy" rating for MSEX with a consensus price target of $60.33.

feed.title: QCR delivers record 2025 income, strong Q4 results
feed.summary: QCR Holdings, Inc. announced record annual net income of $127.2 million for 2025, with diluted EPS of $7.49, alongside strong fourth-quarter results. The company reported quarterly net income of $35.7 million and diluted EPS of $2.12, exceeding analyst estimates. This performance was driven by improved traditional banking, wealth management, and LIHTC lending businesses, along with strategic investments in digital transformation.

feed.title: QCR Holdings Q4 2025 slides: record earnings confirm multi-year growth trajectory
feed.summary: QCR Holdings (NASDAQ: QCRH) reported record fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 earnings, surpassing analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue. The Midwest-based bank holding company highlighted its consistent outperformance compared to peers, driven by a differentiated business model that combines traditional banking with specialty finance and wealth management services. The company projects continued loan growth and capital markets revenue into 2026, building on its strong financial performance and diversified asset base.

feed.title: Truist Financial Corporation (NYSE:TFC) to Issue Quarterly Dividend of $0.52
feed.summary: Truist Financial Corporation (NYSE:TFC) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.52 per share, payable on March 2nd to shareholders of record by February 13th. The dividend represents an annualized yield of 4.1% and is well-covered by earnings, with a payout ratio of 46.5%. Despite missing recent quarterly revenue and EPS estimates, analysts maintain a "Hold" rating on TFC with an average price target of $54.20.

feed.title: Jupiter Neurosciences Expands Nugevia™ Addressable Market with Focus on GLP-1 Users
feed.summary: Jupiter Neurosciences is expanding the market for its Nugevia™ longevity product line to support individuals using GLP-1-based weight loss therapies. The company believes Nugevia™ can address unmet needs related to mitochondrial energy production, lean tissue preservation, and neuro-gut inflammatory balance, which can be stressed during GLP-1-induced weight loss. This strategy combines their clinical-stage pharmaceutical pipeline with a direct-to-consumer longevity business, both powered by their proprietary JOTROL™ resveratrol formulation.

feed.title: Domino's Pizza Faces Tough Comps Ahead Of Q4: Analyst
feed.summary: Guggenheim analyst Gregory Francfort reiterated a Neutral rating on Domino's Pizza (NASDAQ: DPZ) but lowered the price target from $490 to $450 due to anticipated lingering industrywide pressures into the second half of 2026. The analyst decreased earnings forecasts for 2025 and 2026, expecting slower momentum in Q4 due to softer industry demand and external factors. Despite increased U.S. market share since the pandemic and potential benefits from competitor struggles, Francfort cautions that share gains may only partially offset fading promotional benefits.

feed.title: Wells Fargo’s FiNet Recruits Financial Advisors From Ameriprise, Raymond James, UBS
feed.summary: Wells Fargo's independent wealth management channel, FiNet, has successfully recruited financial advisors from Ameriprise, Raymond James, and UBS. These new recruits previously oversaw a combined total of over $1.3 billion in assets at their former firms. This marks a series of recruiting successes for Wells Fargo as it aims to strengthen its FiNet division.

feed.title: Air Products and Chemicals, Inc. (NYSE:APD) Raises Dividend to $1.81 Per Share
feed.summary: Air Products and Chemicals, Inc. (NYSE:APD) has announced a quarterly dividend increase to $1.81 per share, up 1.1% from its previous dividend. The ex-dividend date is April 1st, with the payment scheduled for May 11th. The company has a strong dividend history, having raised its dividend for 43 consecutive years, and analysts expect its earnings to continue covering future dividend payments.

feed.title: Merck & Co., Inc. Plans Quarterly Dividend of $0.85 (NYSE:MRK)
feed.summary: Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.85 per share, payable on April 7th to shareholders of record by March 16th. This translates to an annual dividend yield of 3.2%, with the company having consistently raised its dividend for the past 14 years. Analysts project strong earnings for Merck & Co., Inc. next year, indicating continued ability to cover its dividend payments.

feed.title: Invesco Ltd. (NYSE:IVZ) Plans Quarterly Dividend of $0.21
feed.summary: Invesco Ltd. (NYSE:IVZ) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.21 per share, equating to an annualized dividend of $0.84 and a 3.0% yield. The dividend is set to be paid on March 3rd to shareholders of record as of February 13th. The asset manager recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations with $0.62 EPS and $1.26 billion in revenue, demonstrating the sustainability of its dividend based on its current payout ratio of 46.4%.

feed.title: HP Inc. to Issue Quarterly Dividend of $0.30 (NYSE:HPQ)
feed.summary: HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.30 per share, payable on April 1st to shareholders of record on March 11th. The company maintains a 15-year history of annual dividend increases and a low payout ratio, indicating good coverage by earnings. HP's stock recently traded up slightly, with a market capitalization of $17.85 billion.

feed.title: General Mills, Inc. Announces Quarterly Dividend of $0.61 (NYSE:GIS)
feed.summary: General Mills, Inc. (NYSE:GIS) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.61 per share, payable on May 1st to shareholders of record on April 10th. The company has consistently increased its dividend for the past five years and maintains a sustainable payout ratio. Analysts anticipate General Mills will continue to cover its dividend with future earnings, despite recent stock trading showing minimal change.

feed.title: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:CF) Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.50
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:CF) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share, payable on February 27th to shareholders of record by February 13th. The company has consistently increased its dividend and maintains a conservative payout ratio, with analysts expecting continued coverage by future earnings. Despite trading up 1.1% on the announcement, analysts generally maintain a "Hold" rating on the stock with an average target price of $89.71.

feed.title: Farmers National Banc Corp. to Issue Quarterly Dividend of $0.17 (NASDAQ:FMNB)
feed.summary: Farmers National Banc Corp. (NASDAQ:FMNB) declared a quarterly dividend of $0.17 per share, payable on February 27th to shareholders of record by February 13th, representing an annualized yield of 5.4%. The company has a dividend payout ratio of 43.0% and analysts expect it to cover future dividends with a projected payout ratio of 43.9%. Despite recent market trading down and missing earnings estimates, analysts maintain a "Hold" rating with an average price target of $14.50.

feed.title: MGIC Investment Corporation (NYSE:MTG) to Issue Quarterly Dividend of $0.15
feed.summary: MGIC Investment Corporation (NYSE:MTG) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.15 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $0.60 and a yield of 2.3%. The dividend is payable on March 6th to shareholders of record on February 17th. With a low payout ratio of 19.7% and six consecutive years of dividend increases, the company's dividend appears sustainable, supported by analyst expectations of $2.81 EPS next year.

feed.title: First Trust NASDAQ Clean Edge Green Energy Index Fund (NASDAQ:QCLN) Sets New 1-Year High - What's Next?
feed.summary: The First Trust NASDAQ Clean Edge Green Energy Index Fund (QCLN) reached a new 52-week high of $52.08, closing at $51.41. The fund, which tracks clean energy companies, also increased its quarterly dividend to $0.0442, resulting in an annualized dividend of $0.18 and a 0.3% yield. Despite some institutional investor interest, top-rated analysts suggest other stocks might be better buys than QCLN.
